column1: 1;"grafo";"What is the subject matter of Article 1 of the GDPR Regulation?";"Article 1 lays down rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and rules relating to the free movement of personal data.";"{'answer': 'Article 1 of the GDPR establishes its **subject matter** as rules for the protection of natural persons regarding the processing of personal data and the **free movement of personal data** within the EU. It explicitly states: *‘This Regulation lays down rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and rules relating to the free movement of personal data.’*'
column2: 'full_answer': 'The GDPR’s **Article 1** defines its core scope by establishing two interrelated objectives: (1) **protection of natural persons’ fundamental rights and freedoms**
column3: particularly their right to data protection
column4: and (2) **ensuring the free movement of personal data** within the EU. This dual focus aligns with the Regulation’s foundational principles
column5: as evidenced by its structure across chapters (e.g.
column7: rights of data subjects in Chapter III
column8: and cross-border data transfer mechanisms in Chapter V). The **breadth of protection** extends to all processing activities (automated or manual) involving personal data
column11: criminal law enforcement). The **free movement provision** (Article 1(3)) ensures no restrictions are imposed solely for data protection reasons
column12: reinforcing the EU’s harmonized approach. This duality reflects the GDPR’s **proactive stance** on balancing individual rights with cross-border data flows
column13: a cornerstone of its regulatory framework.'
column14: 'key_snippet': 'This Regulation lays down rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and rules relating to the free movement of personal data.'

column1: 2;"grafo";"What is the material scope of Article 2 of the GDPR Regulation?";"Article 2 of the GDPR Regulation applies to the processing of personal data wholly or partly by automated means and to the processing other than by automated means of personal data which form part of a filing system or are intended to form part of a filing system. It does not apply to the processing of personal data in the course of an activity which falls outside the scope of Union law
column2: by the Member States when carrying out activities which fall within the scope of Chapter 2 of Title V of the TEU
column3: by a natural person in the course of a purely personal or household activity
column4: or by competent authorities for the purposes of the prevention
column6: detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties
column7: including the safeguarding against and the prevention of threats to public security.";"{'answer': 'The material scope of **Article 2 of the GDPR** includes all processing of personal data by automated or non-automated means
column8: except for cases explicitly excluded in point 2: (a) processing in the course of purely personal/household activities
column9: (b) processing by natural persons outside Union law
column10: (c) processing by Member States for criminal law enforcement
column11: (d) processing by public authorities for specific tasks outside Union law. Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 applies to Union institutions’ data processing. **Key exclusion**: Processing by public authorities for criminal law enforcement is not covered by GDPR’s general scope.'
column12: 'full_answer': ""Article 2 of the GDPR establishes the **material scope** of its application by defining what constitutes 'processing of personal data'. The core scope (point 1) covers all automated and non-automated processing of personal data forming part of a filing system or intended to form one. However

column24: 'key_snippet': 'This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data wholly or partly by automated means and to the processing other than by automated means of personal data which form part of a filing system or are intended to form part of a filing system. This Regulation does not apply to: (a) in the course of an activity which falls outside the scope of Union law; (b) by the Member States when carrying out activities which fall within the scope of Chapter 2 of Title V of the TEU; (c) by a natural person in the course of a purely personal or household activity; (d) by competent authorities for the purposes of the prevention
